mechanism_narrative: A rise in the GDP price index signals an increase in aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansionary periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in recessionary periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the industrial production index signals a weakening supply chain, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in recessionary periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the GDP price index signals an increase in aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in recessionary periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the GDP price index signals an increase in aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.25pp in 3 months and HOUST falls < -0.1pp in 2 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansionary periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 1 month and PAYEMS falls < -0.05pp in 1 month
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in recessionary periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
